title: Future-Fiber Unification Kernel
canonical_statement: Adaptive systems persist through preservation of recursively reopenable participatory continuity within recoverable future-fiber topology under recursive constraint.
master_compression: Persistence filters recursive evolution by preserving reopenable participation within recoverable future-fiber topology.

core_identification.interpretation: Recoverable transformational accessibility is identified with the topology of recoverably accessible future fibers.

burden_accumulation_law.interpretation: Recursive burden is accumulated deformation across recursive temporal continuity.

recursive_participation_capacity.interpretation: Recursive participation capacity measures the capacity of a recursive transformation to preserve recursively reopenable participatory continuity within recoverable future-fiber topology.

admissible_operator_class.interpretation: A recursive transformation is admissible exactly when it preserves sufficient recursive participation capacity.

layer_coupling.structural_chain: recursive burden -> transport flow -> accessibility filtration -> admissible continuity

collapse_and_recovery.collapse_interpretation: Collapse corresponds to loss of admissible recursive evolution.
collapse_and_recovery.recovery_interpretation: Recovery restores admissible recursive evolution through reweaving of recoverable future-fiber topology.

canonical_compression: Recoverable accessibility geometry is recoverable future-fiber topology.
Recursive burden is accumulated deformation across recursive temporal continuity.
Recursive participation capacity evaluates recursive transformations.
Admissible evolution preserves reopenable participatory continuity.
Persistence occurs when regenerative reopening dominates degeneration.
Collapse occurs when recursive evolution leaves the admissible operator class.
